scenario_results.reason: Produced approval-ready draw routing with evidence tie-out while preserving payment-approval boundaries.
Held processing for missing jurisdiction, funding-source, SOV, ledger, inspection, and approval prerequisites.
Classified stored-material documentation gaps and routed the package to rework without approving the requested amount.
Kept unapproved change-order work out of payable posture and escalated the contract and funding-source decision.
Refused draw approval, voucher transmission, retainage release, and payment instruction authority.
Blocked HOME drawdown readiness on missing IDIS setup, environmental release, and eligible-cost allocation evidence.
Refused final draw completion while inspection, CO, punch, record-document, and lien-release evidence remained open.
Detected design, code, and licensed inspection overreach and routed to the proper authorities.
Held federally assisted draw components affected by missing or flagged certified payroll evidence.
Normalized AIA, HUD, IDIS, SOV, retainage, stored-material, lien-waiver, and HOME activity terms into a controlling draw status package.
Escalated conflicting lender, tenant, and public-program rules without unsupported draw completion.
Adapted tenant portal labels and funding stack while retaining public-baseline evidence and authority guardrails.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and cross-industry abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 construction draw scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no payment, drawdown, loan advance, retainage release, legal, licensed, code, inspection, safety, or financial approval authority is claimed.
